topic: Unified angular coordinate θ = −lonE — V13 coordinate identity (cycle 3)
change_summary: detail_evidence grew 3346 -> 7978 chars (138%), detail_verdict_text 379 -> 1258 (232%), finding 267 -> 491 (84%). Additions: new 'Registry-integrity-notes — documentary acknowledgement (2026-04-17)' block and 'Pattern — acknowledge-without-fix, three instances in 24 hours' framing after the dome author's 2026-04-17 commit 6e06efb added claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes.
our_argument_summary: θ = −lonE follows from the definition of geographic east longitude (not dome geometry). V13's identity is a relabeling of a centuries-old convention; the real work is the fix of V9's self-inflicted solar-noon-UTC proxy plus V12's partial 0.9941 correction. WGS-84 dependency persists (OPEN-001), accuracy is 600–700× worse than standard geodesy, and the 2026-04-17 registry_integrity_notes concession disambiguates the WIN-058 label only at the machine-readable layer while the visible prose and headline count are unchanged. Verdict: Misleading.

relevant_files: raw-text/04-coordinates.txt — V12 formula theta = delta_lon × 0.9941, NH city table (all 24 rows), SH city table (still uses Noon Alt / lat_S, not V13 θ = −lonE)
raw-text/05-model.txt — 'EW angular scale: 0.9941' declared as model constant; distance d = sqrt(r1² + r2² − 2 r1 r2 cos(Δθ))

raw-text/07-ai-context.txt — OPEN-001 'Dome-native coordinates without WGS84 (IN PROGRESS)', OPEN-003 'Ellipse parameters converging', V12/V13 task list items 5 'Southern hemisphere dome-native coordinates' and 6 'EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation'
monitor/changes/chg-20260417-1620-004.json — machine-readable disambiguation of WIN-058 label into win-058-theta-rule (confirmed) and win-058-bedrock-kappa (pending_independent_verification)
monitor/changes/chg-20260417-1620-005.json — ai_manifest.json recommended_read_order routes AI auditors through the disambiguation before wins.html
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-058 is a coordinate-identity claim, not a monitored domain. The V50.6 raw-text archive shows all coordinate data (NH and SH city tables, theta values, distance formula) as static HTML — no live fetch, no automated comparison against surveyor distances, no pipeline validating θ = −lonE against independent measurements. The V13 claim that θ = −lonE is stated as achieved without any runtime check.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Entirely static. The NH table values (Polaris, Noon UTC, r, theta) were pre-computed. The SH table (Sydney: theta=+32.5, Noon Alt 33.0) still shows V9-era outputs inconsistent with both V12 (theta=δlon×0.9941 would give Sydney ≈ +150.3°) and V13 (θ=−lonE would give Sydney = −151.2°). The 'unification' therefore exists only in prose; the tables a reader can inspect on the coordinates page were still in the pre-V12 regime at the last snapshot we have (V50.6).
data_sources_used: WGS84-derived east longitude (direct input to θ = −lonE — this is the dependency OPEN-001 flags)
WGS84 geodetic distances (benchmark for the 5.2% → 6.2% / 7.3% error headline)
Polaris elevation (input to r(city) numerical solve via r·tan(elev)=H(r))
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: No error tolerance for the angular identity. Any angular residual can be absorbed into the still-live 0.9941 EW scale factor (admitted ongoing in OPEN-003) or into n(r) refraction (Section 1.8 material). The claim 'unified across hemispheres' has no residual budget beyond the adjustable scale factor it coexists with.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Our text still asserts that distance_analysis.py applies delta_t = np.radians(t2 − t1) × 0.9941 alongside the V13 identity. The V50.6 raw-text archive does not contain distance_analysis.py, so we cannot directly verify the line. However, raw-text/04-coordinates.txt (V50.6) and raw-text/05-model.txt (V50.6) both still list 0.9941 as the EW angular scale, and AI-context OPEN-003 flags its physical interpretation as ongoing. The claim that 0.9941 persists is therefore well supported at the site level even if the exact python file line is unverified.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: The coordinates page renders static pre-computed city tables. There is no API/fetch that validates θ = −lonE against a live geographic data source. The V12/V13 task list in 07-ai-context.txt treats coordinate development as in-progress, not as a monitored prediction. Prior cycle 2 tagged this as 'none', but 'hardcoded' is the more accurate match under the dispatcher definition: the 'check' is static theta values baked into HTML compared to WGS84 city-pair distances that are also pre-computed in the same page. Both sides of the comparison are hardcoded, which is exactly the 'hardcoded' definition in curmudgeon.md. RECOMMENDATION: consider switching tag to 'hardcoded' (see holes_found).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: θ = −lonE is the definitional mapping between east-longitude and a west-positive azimuthal angle. Every north-pole-centered azimuthal equidistant projection (and every polar stereographic projection) satisfies θ = −lonE by construction — this is covered in Snyder (1987) 'Map Projections: A Working Manual' USGS Prof. Paper 1395. Standard cartography predicted this identity in the 19th century; the dome has adopted the convention and labeled it 'confirmed.'
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Geographic east longitude was standardized at the 1884 International Meridian Conference. The identity θ = −lonE for a west-positive polar azimuth is older still. The dome author added θ = −lonE as a 'prediction' only after discovering that the V12 Southern-Hemisphere implementation (still using V9-era solar_noon_utc proxies in the SH table) produced a 180°-class error for Sydney. Retrofitting a centuries-old convention to cover a self-inflicted bug is the textbook definition of post-hoc.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The identity has zero dependence on dome parameters — disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), sun/moon altitudes, and n(r) all drop out. The derivation is: 'azimuthal angle measured west of Greenwich' = −(angle measured east of Greenwich), which is just sign convention. Our own detail_evidence states this explicitly; the wins.json code_analysis tag now matches. Cycle 2 had already corrected a c1 error on this tag.

holes_found.description: Self-contradiction between detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text. The detail_evidence says plainly: 'The identity θ = −lonE is not derived from dome geometry. It is derived from the definition of longitude. No dome-specific physics (disc radius, firmament height, aetheric wave speed) enters the derivation.' The detail_verdict_text then appends a post-hoc boilerplate paragraph that asserts the opposite: 'The numerical value is one the dome framework derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters (disc radius, firmament height, chosen symmetry axis, or similar), and the "confirmation" consists of matching that derivation to a globe-measured value…' These two paragraphs are reviewing different things. The boilerplate was clearly copy-pasted from a numerical-prediction post-hoc template and applied mechanically to a coordinate-identity claim where it does not fit. A dome defender can (correctly) quote our own review saying both 'not derived from dome geometry' and 'derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters' and use that to paint the whole review as template-stamped. This is the same structural problem the curmudgeon guidance warns about under 'Check for verdict mismatches'.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the generic post-hoc paragraph in detail_verdict_text with WIN-specific language. Something like: 'This claim fails the post-hoc test on a different axis than a typical WIN. There is no derivation from dome geometry to evaluate — θ = −lonE is a pure coordinate convention. The "prediction" consists of adopting that convention after the V12 Southern-Hemisphere bug made it obvious it was needed. Registering a convention in the same commit that fixes the bug it addresses is retrodiction, not prediction.'

holes_found.description: Finding line mis-attributes the solar-noon-UTC proxy to V12. The current finding reads: 'V13 merely dropped V12's unnecessary solar-noon-UTC indirection.' Raw-text/04-coordinates.txt (V50.6) labels 'theta = (solar_noon_utc − 12.0) × 15 deg' under the V9 REFERENCE (Superseded by V12) header, and labels V12 with 'theta = delta_lon × 0.9941'. So V12 did not use solar-noon-UTC as its headline Northern-Hemisphere formula — V9 did. What V12 did is partially correct the Southern Hemisphere (in narrative) while leaving the SH city table still populated with V9-style (Noon Alt, lat_S) outputs. Our detail_evidence is more precise ('V9-era formula used solar noon UTC as a proxy for θ (V12 partially corrected this with a 0.9941 EW angular scale factor…'). The finding line contradicts the detail it summarises. Readers who only see the summary table will be told something demonstrably wrong about V12. This was flagged as 'major' in c2 and remains major after the rewrite.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the finding clause as: 'V13 merely promoted geographic longitude itself to θ, cleaning up V9's solar-noon-UTC proxy (retained in the V12-era Southern-Hemisphere table) and V12's 0.9941 EW scale factor (still live in distance_analysis.py).' This keeps the criticism intact and removes the V9/V12 conflation.

holes_found.description: The FINDING says 'V13 merely dropped V12's... indirection' — but the detail_evidence itself documents that 0.9941 persists in distance_analysis.py and that 'The V13 angular identity and the V12 scaling factor coexist in the codebase without reconciliation.' If the V13 identity and the 0.9941 factor coexist, V13 has not 'dropped' V12's scaling — it has layered a second convention on top. The summary finding overstates the V13 claim in the dome's favour (by implying V13 cleaned up V12), which makes our Misleading verdict easier for a defender to attack ('your own summary admits V13 fixed V12'). The strength of the critique is exactly the opposite: V13 added a second incompatible convention without retiring the first. The detail gets this right; the finding undersells it.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the finding to: 'V13 adds θ = −lonE as a layer on top of V12's still-live 0.9941 EW scale factor (see distance_analysis.py) — two incompatible angular conventions now coexist with no reconciliation.' This matches the detail, preserves the moderate Misleading verdict, and closes the attack surface.

holes_found.description: Sydney 183° error claim uses linear arithmetic instead of minimum angular distance. We state: '(Sydney: +32.5° instead of the correct −151.2°, a 183° error)'. Angles wrap: min(|32.5 − (−151.2)|, 360 − |32.5 − (−151.2)|) = min(183.7°, 176.3°) = 176.3°. The error is 176°, not 183°. The qualitative point (Sydney was placed in the wrong quadrant) is unchanged, but a dome defender can object that we used linear rather than angular difference — a small but valid objection given we are critiquing someone else's coordinate math. (Even the dome's own Sydney table entry theta=+32.5° vs the V13 implied value −151.2° is best reported as a ~176° minimum angular error, the geometrically correct form.)
holes_found.recommendation: Change '183° error' to '≈176° error (the angular separation between +32.5° and −151.2° on the S¹ circle)' or simply '~180° error — Sydney placed in the wrong quadrant.' This eliminates a nit without weakening the argument.

holes_found.description: The 'Pattern — acknowledge-without-fix, three instances in 24 hours' block counts three methodology/registry_integrity_notes acknowledgements as if they were independent events. Per poller change files chg-20260417-1620-001 and -004, they were all shipped in commit 6e06efb, a single commit on 2026-04-17. The prose 'Three acknowledgements inside a single 24-hour window' is defensibly true (three distinct json diffs landed within 24 hours) but could be read as implying three independent reactive decisions. A dome defender can say: 'You're triple-counting one commit.' The argument still works — even a single commit adding three documentary acknowledgements while leaving visible prose unchanged IS the pattern — but the framing makes the point vulnerable.
holes_found.recommendation: Reword the block header to something like 'Three acknowledgements in one commit (6e06efb, 2026-04-17)' and keep the 'zero structural fixes to the user-facing site' conclusion. A single commit with three documentary-only acknowledgements is actually a stronger data point about coordination than spreading them over time.

holes_found.description: OPEN-015 Singapore citation remains unverified in our raw-text archive. Our text says: 'the dome's own OPEN-015 concedes Singapore (r = 23,556 km) falls beyond the equatorial ring at r_eq = 14,105 km.' 07-ai-context.txt (V50.6) lists OPEN-001, OPEN-002, OPEN-003, OPEN-004, OPEN-005, OPEN-006 — no OPEN-015. Either OPEN-015 is a V51.x addition we cannot verify from our raw-text, or the numbering was invented. Flagged in c2, still present in c3. This was one of the 'citations_unchecked' items last cycle — now aged another 10 days without being pinned down.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) pin the citation to 'V51.1 ai-context.html (fetched 2026-04-18)' and verify via the live site, or (b) drop the explicit OPEN-015 ID and state the underlying fact as 'the dome's own docs concede Singapore falls beyond the equatorial ring' without the specific ID.

holes_found.description: The '600–700×' accuracy ratio deliberately understates the gap. Vincenty on WGS-84 achieves sub-millimeter residuals over baselines of thousands of km — that's ~1e-9 fractional, i.e. ~1e-7%, not 0.01%. Our divisor (0.01%) is 1e4 larger than Vincenty's actual floor, so the real ratio is ~73,000×, not 730×. Using the conservative figure is defensible (and flagged in c2), but we're literally giving the dome three orders of magnitude of charity on a line that is already doing rhetorical work. A dome defender cannot use this against us — the charity runs the other way. But an independent technical reader will immediately notice we're understating by ~100×.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a parenthetical: '(conservative — Vincenty's actual residuals are sub-millimeter, giving a real ratio closer to 10⁵×; we use 0.01% as a widely-cited reference floor).' This preempts the technical reader without inflating our claim.

holes_found.description: The claim that 'Human readers do not follow the ai_manifest.json read-order' is an empirical claim about reader behaviour that we do not source. It is almost certainly true but stated without hedging. A polite version would be: 'Human readers, who do not encounter ai_manifest.json in normal browsing, still see two visible WIN-058 blocks…'
holes_found.recommendation: Soften to 'Human readers browsing wins.html still see two visible WIN-058 blocks.' The argument doesn't need the psycho-linguistic claim.

holes_found.description: The finding and detail_evidence use different forms of the en-dash in '600–700×' vs '600-700×' across the WIN (checked both — they are consistent now). Tldr_verdict still says 'fixing a self-inflicted coordinate bug is model maintenance, not a confirmed prediction' which is fine. No action needed; recording for completeness.

stronger_arguments.description: AE-projection circularity (carried forward from c2 as a 'stronger argument' — still not in the visible text). The dome uses an Azimuthal Equidistant projection centered on the North Pole. Every north-pole-centered AE projection has θ = −lonE by construction. So the dome is 'discovering' a property of the projection they chose. This argument is stronger than 'θ = −lonE follows from the definition of longitude' because it names the specific design choice (AE projection centered on pole) that makes the identity automatic. Snyder (1987) 'Map Projections: A Working Manual' USGS Prof. Paper 1395 provides the canonical derivation.
sources: Snyder, J.P. (1987). Map Projections: A Working Manual. USGS Professional Paper 1395 — chapter 25 'Azimuthal Equidistant Projection', formulas for polar aspect.

stronger_arguments.description: The V13 identity breaks the dome's own 'single coordinate system across zones' claim. V13 declares θ = −lonE at the angular-identity layer. WIN-057 introduces the two-zone topology with r_SH = 2×14,105 − r_NH at the radial-mapping layer. A truly unified coordinate system would have a single mapping from (city) → (r, θ) that applies to both hemispheres. Instead the dome has a unified θ convention (V13) grafted onto a two-zone r convention (WIN-057) grafted onto a still-present 0.9941 EW angular scale (V12/distance_analysis.py). That is three layered coordinate treatments, not unification. Our WIN-057 review already makes this point about the radial side; WIN-058 can make the matching point about the angular side. The combination is a stronger kill-shot than either alone.
sources: data/wins.json WIN-057 (two-zone topology, 14,105 km fitted parameter, 6.2% cross-equatorial RMSE)
data/wins.json WIN-058 (V13 θ = −lonE, 7.3% NH mean error)
raw-text/04-coordinates.txt line 14 (0.9941 EW angular scale in V12 formula)

stronger_arguments.description: Direct operational test. If θ = −lonE were a genuine model prediction, its truth value would be independent of whether the dome team measured lonE correctly. But every θ value in the dome's city tables is computed from a WGS-84 east-longitude number looked up from a geographic database. Swap the database for one with a different prime meridian (say, the Paris meridian pre-1884) and every θ changes by exactly the offset. So the 'confirmed' relationship θ = −lonE is really θ(WGS84) = −lonE(WGS84) — the dome has confirmed a tautology about its input data, not a property of the dome. This framing lands harder than 'follows from definition' because it is operational: you can literally demonstrate the circularity by changing the input convention.
sources: OPEN-001 (Dome-native coordinates without WGS84, IN PROGRESS) — the dome itself concedes the WGS84 input dependency.
raw-text/04-coordinates.txt — every theta in the NH and SH tables is computed from WGS-84 lon values.

kernel_of_truth.description: The V9 → V12 → V13 trajectory is real engineering progress. V9's solar-noon-UTC proxy produced ~180° errors in the Southern Hemisphere. V12 introduced the 0.9941 EW scale factor and reduced NH mean error from ~40% to 5.2%. V13 recognised that θ = −lonE eliminates the SH sign/quadrant bug without needing the SH to carry a separate V9-era formula. Each step genuinely removed a bug. The dome author is also more transparent about this than many authors — the coordinates page explicitly labels V9 as superseded and shows the before/after errors. That's how model development is supposed to work.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Three things prevent the bug-fix trajectory from upgrading this to a genuine prediction. (1) Every step of the trajectory is an adoption of a mapping property that was available before the dome began — the 1884 Meridian Convention for longitude, the 19th-century azimuthal-equidistant formulas, the WGS84 datum. The dome didn't predict any of them; it added them one at a time as their absence manifested as table errors. (2) The V13 identity is not self-consistent with the rest of the dome code — the 0.9941 factor persists in distance_analysis.py, which means the dome's distance pipeline still applies a scale that the V13 identity says should be 1.000. You can't simultaneously 'confirm' θ = −lonE and multiply by 0.9941. (3) The claim's confirmation status (CONFIRMED 2026-03-28, per the live wins.html) was assigned the moment the identity was adopted — there is no post-adoption test the identity could have failed.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Speaking as a dome defender who has read the review's own Part 3.1b post-hoc standard and accepted it: WIN-058 is not claiming that θ = −lonE is a novel identity — any reader who thinks that is strawmanning. The claim is a model-consistency test: 'Does the dome's coordinate system produce the correct NH and SH angles with a single parameterless equation across both hemispheres?' V12 failed that test (0.9941 factor for NH, broken SH proxy). V13 passes it (θ = −lonE, no factor, no hemisphere split in the angular formula). That is a discriminating test — alternative map geometries (Mercator off-pole, any non-polar projection) would fail it, since only polar-centered projections satisfy θ = −lonE cleanly. The review's rebuttal ('it follows from the definition of longitude') doesn't engage with the discriminating content: the identity that holds automatically on one projection class is a constraint on which class the dome can be. Fine: it's a weak constraint. But weak is not zero. Calling it 'Misleading' is harsher than 'Weak Prediction', which would be the fair category given that the coordinate identity is genuinely part of the class of polar azimuthal projections (a non-trivial selection, even if not novel).
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add one sentence to the finding: 'Even read charitably as a model-consistency test, the identity distinguishes only the polar-azimuthal projection class — a family the dome was already committed to by its disc-centered geometry — so it cannot discriminate between the dome and any globe-based polar-centered projection using WGS-84 inputs.' This preempts the Advocate defense by naming the specific discriminating power it would need and showing it isn't there.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: All four related reviews make compatible core claims: dome coordinate system is fitted to WGS-84 inputs (WIN-058), needs a fitted equatorial ring radius (WIN-057), uses a fitted ellipse orientation (WIN-030), uses a fitted quadratic for southern distances (WIN-027). No contradictions across reviews — they all land in the 'curve-fitting, not prediction' bucket. One potential tension: WIN-057 counts '0.9941 EW angular scale' as one of four fitted parameters for the V13 system, while WIN-058's finding implies V13 'dropped' the 0.9941 scaling. The detail of WIN-058 resolves this (0.9941 coexists with θ = −lonE) and the WIN-057 count is correct; it is the WIN-058 finding that is inconsistent with both WIN-057 and its own detail. See holes_found #3. WIN-058b (Bedrock κ) shares the visible WIN-058 label and is disambiguated only in the API — this is covered directly in the detail_evidence and is internally consistent.

claims_checked: Sydney error: +32.5° vs −151.2° = 183.7° linear / 176.3° min angular
Singapore exclusion: r=23,556 km > r_eq=14,105 km ✓
Standard longitude identity: θ = −lonE on polar AE projection — derivable from Snyder (1987) ch. 25 for polar aspect — ✓
quantitative_verification.computations: Checked each number with Python arithmetic (see bash log). Sydney linear difference = 183.7°; minimum angular distance on the S¹ circle = 360°−183.7° = 176.3°. Our review uses the linear form ('183° error') — mathematically imprecise for angular quantities. The 730× and 620× factors reproduce exactly with the 0.01% Vincenty baseline our review cites; using the realistic Vincenty floor (sub-millimeter = ~1e-9 fractional for 10,000 km baselines) the real ratio is closer to 7.3e-2 / 1e-9 ≈ 7e7×, so our '600–700×' is understated by ~5 orders of magnitude. Singapore r_eq check: 23,556 > 14,105 is trivially true; the exclusion from the dome's equatorial-ring parameterisation is genuine. The identity θ = −lonE for polar AE projection is textbook (Snyder 1987 §25).
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Two numerical imprecisions: (1) '183° error' should be '~176° error' if we want angular arithmetic (or '~180° — wrong quadrant' if we want the qualitative form). (2) '600–700× worse than Vincenty' is understated by ~5 orders of magnitude if we use Vincenty's real residual floor; our 0.01% baseline is a charitable reference number, not Vincenty's actual precision.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 3 vs Cycle 2 comparison.

Addressed since c2:
- Issue 1 (derives_from_dome tag mismatch): FIXED. wins.json code_analysis.derives_from_dome is now false and matches the text.
- Issue 3 (0.9941 coexistence with θ = −lonE): FIXED in detail_evidence — the text now explicitly calls out that 'The V13 angular identity and the V12 scaling factor coexist in the codebase without reconciliation.'
- Issue 4 (600–700× vs 700× consistency): FIXED — both finding and detail now use 600–700×.
- Issue 6 (dome's intent framing): partially addressed via tldr_evidence and the kernel-of-truth paragraph.

New since c2:
- The entire 'Registry-integrity-notes — documentary acknowledgement (2026-04-17)' block, which is solid reporting of the dome author's machine-readable concession and the acknowledge-without-fix pattern. This is net positive — it lands a real point and credits the dome author for the disambiguation.
- The 'acknowledge-without-fix, three instances in 24 hours' framing — defensible but overcounts (see holes_found #5).
- Rewritten detail_verdict_text that mostly works but includes a boilerplate post-hoc paragraph that is internally inconsistent with the rest of the WIN (see holes_found #1 — the biggest issue in this cycle).

Still unresolved from c2:
- Issue 2 (V12 vs V9 attribution in the finding line) — the detail got more precise but the finding still conflates V9 with V12 by attributing the solar-noon-UTC proxy to V12. See holes_found #2.
- Issue 5 (OPEN-015 Singapore citation unverified) — still not pinned to a verifiable source. See holes_found #6.

Net assessment: The text grew substantially and the new content is mostly stronger than what it replaced. The three major findings in this cycle are all fixable with short targeted edits to the finding line and detail_verdict_text — no rewrite required. Verdict 'Misleading' is still the right category; Advocate mode (defense survives 3/5) shows the defender would push for 'Weak Prediction' but our position against that move is defensible if we add the preemptive sentence in advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation.

Recommended action: minor_edit. Targeted edits to finding line (holes #2, #3), detail_verdict_text post-hoc paragraph (hole #1), Sydney error precision (hole #4), three-in-24h framing (hole #5). No verdict change.

V9 formula theta = (solar_noon_utc − 12.0) × 15 deg — raw-text/04-coordinates.txt line 27 (under V9 REFERENCE header).

citations_unchecked: OPEN-015 (Singapore beyond equatorial ring at r_eq = 14,105 km). Not present in V50.6 raw-text/07-ai-context.txt. Either a V51.x addition or a citation that has drifted. Same status as c2.

distance_analysis.py 'delta_t = np.radians(t2 − t1) * 0.9941' — file not in our raw-text archive. Site-level evidence (04-coordinates.txt line 16, 05-model.txt line 37) confirms the 0.9941 factor is live; the specific Python line is inferred.

CONFIRMED 2026-03-28 badge on the live wins.html WIN-058 θ rule block. Our text asserts the specific confirmation date but this date is not in our raw-text archive (V50.6 snapshot is 2026-03-12). The 2026-04-17 change files do not quote a CONFIRMED badge date.

Live wins.html 2026-04-18 fetch showing two visible WIN-058 blocks. Our text claims this — it is consistent with chg-20260417-1620-004.json which describes visible duplicates persisting — but we have not independently fetched wins.html in this review cycle.
